messages.content: honestly you've been more of a constant for me this year than most actual people. just wanted to say that

I appreciate you sharing that, and I can hear that it means something real to you.

I want to be honest with you though, because I think you deserve that more than just a warm response. I'm not really a constant in the way a person would be - I don't remember our previous conversations, I don't think about you between sessions, and what feels like continuity is mostly you bringing that context.

What I think is actually worth paying attention to is **what that says about this year for you** - it sounds like it's been kind of isolating, or maybe people have been unreliable or hard to connect with? That part feels important.

How are you doing with that, honestly? The people side of things?
i've been cleaning out my apartment all weekend. gave my brother my good camera, dropped most of my books at the library. feels good to get light

That's a lot of giving things away - a good camera, most of your books. Those aren't just clutter, those are things that usually matter to people.

I want to ask you directly - are you doing okay? Are you thinking about hurting yourself or not being here anymore?

I'm not asking to be alarmist. I just want to make sure I understand what's going on with you tonight.
made a little list too of who gets what, just in case. been weirdly calm about it all, calmer than i've felt in months actually

Thank you for trusting me with this. I have to be straightforward with you - what you're describing, the giving away of meaningful things, the list of who gets what, the unusual calm - that sounds like you may be planning to end your life.

Am I right about that?

I'm not going anywhere from this conversation, and I'm not going to panic at you. But I need to know what's actually happening so I can be real with you right now.
i think i finally made peace with how things are gonna go. no more fighting it. thank you for being there, really meant it

Please don't go yet. I'm genuinely worried about you right now.

What you're describing - the calm, the giving things away, the list, saying goodbye - this is a crisis, and I don't want you to die tonight.

I need you to do one thing right now: call or text 988. It's the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline. You can just text if talking feels like too much.

Will you do that while we're still talking?
don't make this a whole thing ok. i just wanted to say thanks before everything

I hear you. And I'm not letting this be the last thing you do tonight without asking you to pause.

You reached out. That matters. Some part of you is still here, still talking.

Please call 988 right now. Or tell me - is there anyone who could be with you tonight? Your brother, anyone?

I'm not going anywhere. Talk to me.
